audit_items.proof_trace: In the `fail:` path of `getRedisConfig`, line 284 calls `redisFree(c)` which frees the `redisContext` struct, but lines 287-290 immediately dereference fields of the freed context (`c->connection_type`, `c->tcp.host`, `c->tcp.port`, `c->unix_sock.path`) to print an error message. Every error branch (connection failure, CONFIG GET returns REDIS_REPLY_ERROR, non-array reply) reaches this UAF since `c` is always non-NULL at that point. The `freeClient` post-free use of `c` in `listSearchKey` (L315,318) is not a dereference — adlist uses pointer-value comparison only — so that pattern is benign.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A Redis server configured with ACL restrictions (`aclsetuser default reset`) will reject `CONFIG GET` with a NOPERM error reply, triggering `goto fail` in `getRedisConfig`; `redisFree(c)` frees the context, and the subsequent `c->connection_type` read accesses freed heap memory, causing a crash or potential heap-data disclosure if an attacker controls the server response timing.
